author: @Gwydion67
text:
Thank your so much for explaining, Rob! 😃👍
Of course I'll subscribe to both your new channel and to Luke's own.
Unfortunately enough, I needed a break for the last 1-2 years, reducing my screen time on YouTube, shifting priorities a bit among my 90+ subscriptions.
Even my ADHD superpower has its limits... 😉

Regards,
Gwydion / Marc

Regardless, I've subscribed to all 3 and will continue to watch your excellent content 👍

Watched now .... woo good luck to luke ..

Tin cans 
Fly screen gauze.

author: @livingladolcevita7318
text: Subscribed to them Rob!! I looked at your power/energy saving vlog about a year ago now and added some ideas. I had a quick look but couldn't find anything but wondering if you have covered anything about using solar panels to power an immersion heater/ tank of water directly. I am considering doing this and yes I know might take all day or longer to heat up but will only use this water for the occasional shower. Perhaps there may be a better alternative. Good luck to Luke and as Swansea is just down the road from me I'll say hello if I meet him.

author: @marcfruchtman9473

author: @AutoNomades
text: Hello, I like the idea to group the subjects together, but less to make an hour video of it, when i'm in front of a long one, i don't watch often... Why not just doing the same as before, but making One playlist for ideas and toughts, one for whatever tech a, the other for whatever tech b, c, d, and etc... It is more easy to find back the chunk you're looking for when needed.. There are also the timelines with pictures AND titles, that can be helpfull..; Otherwise it such a pain .. You can produce one video by day, but who can spend (and keep attention on vlog format.), one hour long .. ?

author: @8ank3r

Cheers and keep inspiring all of us to MAKE something.

author: @MetalAsFork
text:
Done and done. 

I'm sure you get this question all the time, and the answer depends on the context, but I have an idea in my head for a product/invention but I don't know what the right path is to make it real.

Forgive me if this is common knowledge as I've only found your content recently, but have you brought any products to the market? I can only assume you have. 

The thing is, I'd like to start making prototypes and just tinkering, but then I watch your videos and feel like I'd be wasting my time, and that I ought to partner up with someone that really has a better grasp on engineering and product design...

Any advice?
